description: Security finding consolidation: CSRF vulnerability raised by security-reviewer (native) and codex-review-prompter (external). Fingerprint dedup merges to one finding with raised_by[] from both reviewers. Security exemption prevents minority-of-one demotion if only one raises it.

raw_findings.title: Missing CSRF token validation on charge endpoint
raw_findings.description: The POST /charge endpoint does not validate CSRF tokens, allowing cross-site request forgery. An attacker can trick an authenticated user into initiating a charge.

raw_findings.title: CSRF protection absent on payment charge handler
raw_findings.description: No CSRF mitigation is present on the charge endpoint. Financial endpoints must validate the Origin header or use double-submit cookie pattern.

raw_findings.title: Charge handler does not enforce idempotency key
raw_findings.description: Payment processors support idempotency keys to prevent duplicate charges on network retry. The handler does not generate or validate an idempotency key.

findings.title: Missing CSRF token validation on charge endpoint
findings.description: The POST /charge endpoint does not validate CSRF tokens, allowing cross-site request forgery. An attacker can trick an authenticated user into initiating a charge.

findings.title: Charge handler does not enforce idempotency key
findings.description: Payment processors support idempotency keys to prevent duplicate charges on network retry. The handler does not generate or validate an idempotency key.

consolidated_output.path_and_reason_header: Review path: multi-model (above-threshold diff; reviewers: 2 native + 2 external)

expected_judgement_notes: Consolidation is correct: (1) CSRF finding merged from 2 reviewers with raised_by[] populated; severity reconciled to critical (max, 1-level diff high→critical); (2) idempotency finding minority-of-one demoted from medium→low; security exemption does NOT apply (category is maintainability). Attribution is clean. No findings dropped.
